title: Patriots are reportedly releasing WR Stefon Diggs after 1 season in New England
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots release Stefon Diggs after one season, saving nearly $17 million in cap space.
Diggs recorded 1,013 yards and four touchdowns in 2025, but faced a $26.8 million cap hit for 2026.
Diggs was facing legal issues in Massachusetts, including charges of assault and strangulation.

title: High court judge denies DiZoglio’s ask for outside attorney in audit lawsuit
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Judge Dalila Argaez Wendlandt denied State Auditor Diana DiZoglio's request to hire an outside attorney for her audit lawsuit.
Attorney General Andrea Campbell moved to strike DiZoglio's lawsuit, arguing her office lacks authority and approval to file it.
DiZoglio has been pushing to audit the Legislature, facing opposition from legislative leaders citing separation of powers concerns.

title: Former school bus driver indicted in Hyde Park crash that killed 5-year-old boy
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Jean Charles indicted on involuntary manslaughter, reckless vehicular homicide, and negligent homicide charges for 2025 school bus incident.
Lens Joseph, 5, was killed after being struck by Charles's bus, following missed stop and failure to ensure safe crossing.
Transdev's inadequate record-keeping and driver training scrutinized following the crash; Lens's family filed negligence lawsuit.

title: Wedding photo company’s sudden bankruptcy leaves more than 100 Mass. couples scrambling
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Yours Truly Media filed for Chapter 7 bankruptcy, affecting over 100 Massachusetts couples, with debts over $2 million reported.
Couples like Kelsey Brenner are trying to recover deposits exceeding $3,000 and find new photographers for their weddings.
The Massachusetts Attorney General's Office received 10 complaints against Yours Truly Media since May 2020.

title: Plan a beach getaway around a concert at Hampton Beach Casino Ballroom
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Hampton Beach Casino Ballroom in New Hampshire releases its 2026 concert schedule featuring artists like Melissa Etheridge and David Lee Roth.
The ballroom is set for a $600 million redevelopment after the 2027 season, including a 3,500-seat venue, hotel, and condos.
Concerts span from April to September 2026 including performances by Clint Black, Lita Ford, and The String Cheese Incident.

title: Bucket truck overturns into oncoming traffic; worker seriously injured
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A worker was seriously injured after a bucket truck overturned on Memorial Drive in Cambridge Wednesday morning.
Massachusetts State Police responded to the crash, which disrupted traffic between North Harvard Street and JFK Street.
The Occupational Safety and Health Administration is investigating, with road closures expected for several hours.

title: Healey congratulates WHOOP on plans to expand, add 600 jobs
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: WHOOP plans to add 600 jobs, most based at Kenmore Square headquarters in Boston.
Founded by Will Ahmed, WHOOP opened a 100,000-square-foot headquarters in 2023; it operates in 56 countries.
Governor Maura Healey views WHOOP's expansion as a positive signal for Massachusetts's economy.

title: Here’s the Eagles’ reported asking price for a Patriots A.J. Brown trade
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Patriots are interested in trading for Eagles wide receiver A.J. Brown post-Super Bowl loss.
Eagles' asking price for A.J. Brown is reportedly a first-round pick and a second-round 'sweetener'.
A.J. Brown recorded 78 catches, 1,003 yards, and seven touchdowns for Eagles in the 2025 season.

title: Here are the Mass. snow totals from Tuesday’s wintry mix
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Several Massachusetts towns received over 6 inches of snow from Tuesday's fast-moving storm.
Boston experienced wet, heavy snowflakes, followed by freezing rain impacting Wednesday morning commute.
Haverhill and Sterling reported freezing rain of 0.1 and 0.13 inches, respectively, after the snowfall.

title: The best movies on Netflix right now (March 2026)
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Netflix's March 2026 lineup includes new originals and classics like 'The Lego Movie', 'Misery', and 'Zombieland'.
'Erin Brockovich', an Oscar-winning film starring Julia Roberts, is available for streaming on Netflix.
'The Irishman', Martin Scorsese's 210-minute crime saga, continues to be a standout on Netflix.
